questions.question_text: How many classroom hours of pre-license education must a Connecticut salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Connecticut requires 60 classroom hours of Real Estate Principles and Practices from a DCP-approved school for a salesperson license.

questions.question_text: A candidate has completed their 60-hour pre-license course and is ready to take the exam. What is the PSI exam fee?
questions.explanation: The PSI examination fee is $59, payable when scheduling the exam. The separate $80 is the salesperson exam application fee under CGS § 20-314(c); after passing, the initial license fee is paid to DCP.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age requirement for a Connecticut real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Applicants must be at least 18 years old to apply for a Connecticut real estate salesperson license. This is the standard minimum age in most states.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate passes the national portion but fails the state portion. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Retake only the state portion within 1 year
distractors: Retake only the state portion within 6 months
questions.explanation: Connecticut uses a split exam — failing one portion requires retaking only the failed portion. The candidate must pass both portions within 1 year of eligibility.

questions.question_text: What is the application fee for a Connecticut salesperson license?
questions.explanation: The salesperson license application fee is $80, submitted to DCP. The $59 is the PSI exam fee, and $120 is the broker application fee.

questions.question_text: A salesperson renews their Connecticut real estate license on May 31, 2026. When will that license next expire?
questions.explanation: PA 23-84 (effective April 1, 2024) made real estate licenses biennial (CGS §§ 20-314(f), 20-319). Salesperson licenses expire May 31 of even-numbered years; broker licenses expire November 30.

questions.question_text: A broker wants to obtain a broker's license in Connecticut. In addition to education requirements, how many years of active salesperson experience are needed?
questions.explanation: CGS § 20-314(d): three years' active experience under a supervising licensee (at least 1,500 hours and four closed transactions), plus 60 hours Principles & Practices, 15 hours Legal Compliance, 15 hours Brokerage Principles & Practices, and two 15-hour electives (waived with 20 transactions in five years).

questions.question_text: An associate broker in Connecticut is BEST described as a licensed broker who:
questions.explanation: An associate broker (created by PA 23-84) is a licensed broker who works for another broker in a capacity similar to a salesperson. The supervising broker must know of and consent to the associate broker's activities.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's license expired 30 months ago. Under current law, what is the licensee's reinstatement option?
distractors: Cannot reinstate; must start over completely
May reinstate only with broker sponsorship
questions.explanation: PA 23-84 allows reinstatement within 3 years of expiration. At 30 months (2.5 years), the licensee is still within the reinstatement window and can renew without retaking the exam.

questions.question_text: A Connecticut salesperson must hold their license under which type of licensee?
questions.explanation: A salesperson license must be held under a sponsoring/employing broker. Salespersons cannot practice independently — they must work under broker supervision.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Connecticut licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Connecticut requires 12 hours of CE per 2-year renewal cycle: 3 mandatory hours on a rotating topic plus 9 elective hours.

questions.question_text: Of the 12 CE hours required per cycle, how many are mandatory (on a topic chosen by the state)?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 12 CE hours must be in a mandatory course on a topic that rotates each cycle (e.g., preventing racial bias, CT agency law). The remaining 9 hours are elective.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's license renewal is due May 31, 2026. By what date must CE be completed?
questions.explanation: Effective with 2026 renewals, CE must be completed 90 days before renewal. For salespersons renewing May 31, 2026, the CE deadline is February 28, 2026.

questions.question_text: A licensee misses the CE deadline but completes the required CE one month later. What fee must the licensee pay under PA 23-84?
questions.explanation: CGS § 20-319(e) (PA 23-84): completing missed CE within two months after the license period ends costs $315; more than two but within four months costs $625. The $5,000 figure is the maximum disciplinary fine per violation (§ 20-320).

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of a mandatory CE topic in Connecticut?
questions.correct_answer: Preventing racial bias in real estate
questions.explanation: The mandatory 3-hour CE course covers a rotating topic chosen by the state. Recent mandatory topics have included 'Understanding and Preventing Racial Bias in Real Estate' and 'CT Real Estate Agency Law.'
